rationale: Both correctly diagnose the base-tokenizer/extended-ratio-token crash, cite the same file/lines, and confirm the same DiT-config workaround FayeSpica validated. X is cleaner: it gives a concrete guard-code fix, correctly reads Gaohan123's request as closing this bug with a follow-up tracking issue, and stays grounded in thread facts. Y pads its answer with fabricated 'Preconditions' (specific GPU/NPU memory claims not in the thread or issue), sets disposition to 'keep-open' which sits awkwardly against the maintainer's redirect-to-new-issue signal, includes a placeholder/broken comment link, and duplicates the root-cause explanation across raw pipeline-artifact sections rather than presenting one coherent maintainer response.
